svg.title: HyperWeave receipt artifact
svg.desc: A receipt artifact, using raw genome.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: The same receipt/1 economics rendered as the universally-recognised CVS till receipt: a 300x835 cream register tape (~9:25 to trigger the disproportion gag) where the joke is legible before any number is read. Tools are line items priced in tokens, the model split is the payment tender, failed calls are VOID returns — one printed proof of one agent run, made to be screenshotted.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Vertical y-cursor on ~12% thermal margins (rails x18/x282, centre x150): centred header lockup over left-justified itemisation (POS layout); TOOL QTY/TOKENS columns with '% of basket' sublines and a '+N OTHERS' collapse when the list overflows 5 rows; a per-tool VOID/FAILED breakdown sorted by error count; the IN/OUT/CACHED/WRITTEN/SUBTOTAL ledger; TOTAL DUE; the TENDERED models. The barcode + footer ride the fixed optical base (y773/ y807) and the feed-edge curl fills the blank tape above. Monospace throughout; the thermal-red accent marks only VOIDs.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Adaptation is removed entirely — the raw genome declares no light_mode, so no prefers-color-scheme swap ships: a dark thermal print destroys the CVS recognition that is the whole memetic payload, so the paper stays cream on any host ground. The height is fixed at 835 regardless of content (the disproportion IS the gag), so a short session leaves more blank tape rather than shrinking. The promotional meme filler is dropped — every line binds to real session data, never fabricated coupons.
